Title: Breakdown AI & Search
Description: Deliver three services that transform ScriptOS into an intelligent production platform: (1) the Breakdown Service, which auto-detects production elements (characters, locations, props, wardrobe, VFX, stunts) from the Script AST using NLP within 30 seconds of every checkpoint event; (2) the AI Service, which provides WGA Article 72-compliant dialogue suggestions, format corrections, and consistency checks with full ledger tracking, company consent gating, Anthropic prompt caching, and org-level token quotas; and (3) the Search Service, which indexes all script content in Elasticsearch 8.9+ for sub-500ms hybrid BM25 + vector search with native RRF. This epic concludes at the Alpha milestone (Week 14, Sprint 6 end): 10 external writers invited to use the editor, Series Bible, and AI suggestions. Reference: Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 6, Wiki/05-ai-governance.md, Wiki/08-production-modules.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Breakdown elements auto-detected for 5 test scenes with ≥ 80% F1 vs hand-tagged ground truth
- Breakdown re-triggered within 30s of script.checkpointed NATS event for changed scenes
- AI dialogue_suggestion end-to-end: consent → context assembly → Anthropic API → character guard → ledger write → suggestions displayed
- AI ledger DELETE trigger verified: deletion attempt raises exception at DB level
- Professional org quota of 500K tokens enforced; 429 returned with Retry-After on exhaustion
- Search results returned within 500ms (P95) for a 100-scene test script on staging
- Breakdown Service, AI Service, Search Service all deployed to staging with health checks passing
- Alpha milestone: 10 external writers onboarded with editor + bible + AI access

Title: Breakdown Service — NLP Element Detection and Production Catalog
Description: Auto-detect production elements from Script AST checkpoint events and expose a version-locked catalog. NLP pipeline identifies characters (matched against Bible stubs), locations (parsed from scene headings), and six other categories (props, wardrobe, VFX, stunts, animals, vehicles) using named entity recognition and keyword matching. Every detection carries a confidence score. The catalog is version-controlled and immutable after the publish-saga acquires the draft lock. Exposes gRPC methods for the Scheduling Service and Temporal publish-saga. Reference: Wiki/08-production-modules.md, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 6.
Acceptance Criteria:
- NLP detection runs within 30s of script.checkpointed for all changed scenes
- extractBreakdownFromVersion gRPC callable by Temporal publish-saga; idempotent on same scriptVersionId
- Locked breakdown returns 409 FAILED_PRECONDITION on any mutation attempt
- Breakdown GraphQL subgraph federated; scene { breakdownElements { name category confidence } } resolves
Parent: Breakdown AI & Search

Title: AI Service — WGA-Compliant Dialogue Suggestions and AI Governance
Description: Build the AI Service with WGA Article 72 compliance at its core: company consent model, per-feature writer opt-in, context assembler using Bible and timeline data with prompt caching, model router (Claude Haiku/Sonnet/Opus by feature tier), Anthropic SDK integration, immutable AI ledger with DB trigger, dialogue_suggestion and format_correction features, and Redis-based org token quota + per-user soft limits. All interactions are logged. format_correction is always-on (Tier 1, no consent). dialogue_suggestion requires company consent + writer opt-in (Tier 2). Reference: Wiki/05-ai-governance.md, ADR-020, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 6.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Company consent gate: generative features blocked when ai.company_consents has no active record for org
- dialogue_suggestion end-to-end in < 5s on Professional plan
- Ledger immutability: DB trigger blocks DELETE; entries are permanent
- Prompt cache: second call with same Bible context shows cache_read_input_tokens > 0
- Rate limiting: 429 with Retry-After when org quota exhausted; X-RateLimit-Warning header at 90%
Tags: ai; wga; consent; ledger; anthropic; sprint-6
Parent: Breakdown AI & Search

Title: Search Service — Elasticsearch Hybrid Indexing and Query
Description: Configure Elasticsearch 8.9+ script_content index with BM25 text fields and dense_vector field (OpenAI text-embedding-3-large, 3072 dims). Index updated within 60s of every script.checkpointed event via NATS consumer. Hybrid queries combine BM25 and ANN using Elasticsearch native Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF). Search GraphQL subgraph federated into Apollo Router. Reference: Wiki/10-search.md, ADR-005, ADR-024, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 6.
Acceptance Criteria:
- script_content index created with correct BM25 mappings and dense_vector field
- Scene text reindexed within 60s of checkpoint event
- Hybrid RRF search returns results in < 500ms (P95) for 100-scene script on staging
- Semantic query without exact keywords returns correct scene in top 3 (human eval)
- Search GraphQL subgraph federated; searchScripts query resolves correctly
Parent: Breakdown AI & Search

Title: As a producer I can see auto-detected breakdown elements for every scene so I can verify and edit them without manual data entry
Description: Manual breakdown entry takes 2-4 days for a feature film. Auto-detection from the Script AST should reduce that to hours. The breakdown panel shows elements grouped by category (Cast, Locations, Props, Wardrobe, VFX, Stunts) with confidence badges. Low-confidence detections are highlighted for human review. After a publish-saga lock, elements are frozen. Reference: Wiki/08-production-modules.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Breakdown panel shows auto-detected elements per scene grouped by category
- HIGH badge (≥ 0.9) green; MEDIUM (0.7-0.9) amber; LOW (< 0.7) red
- Elements refresh within 30s after a checkpoint event
- After publish lock: elements show padlock; add/edit/delete controls hidden
- F1 ≥ 80% on 5 test scenes vs hand-tagged ground truth
Parent: Breakdown Service — NLP Element Detection and Production Catalog

Title: As a 1st AD I can manually add edit and remove breakdown elements per scene to correct auto-detections
Description: NLP is imperfect. A 1st AD must correct missed elements, edit mis-categorised detections, and delete false positives. Manual edits persist through re-detection runs — is_manual = true elements are never overwritten by the NLP pipeline.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Manual add: creates element with is_manual = true, confidence = 1.0; persists through re-detection
- Edit: saves immediately with optimistic UI update; rollback on gRPC error
- Delete: removes after confirmation; undo snackbar for 5 seconds
- Version-locked elements: add/edit/delete returns 409; UI disables all controls
- Manual elements show pen icon to distinguish from NLP detections
Parent: Breakdown Service — NLP Element Detection and Production Catalog

Title: As a writer I can request AI dialogue suggestions with WGA-compliant consent and full ledger tracking
Description: WGA Article 72 is non-negotiable. Company must enable AI features. Writer must opt in per feature. Every request and every writer action is written to the immutable AI ledger with prompt hash and modification delta. Character voice forbidden_words checked before returning suggestions. Reference: Wiki/05-ai-governance.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- First use: consent banner shown; toggle stores ai.feature_consents row
- Cmd+Shift+A triggers: consent check → context assembly → Claude Sonnet call → character guard → ledger write → 2-3 suggestions displayed
- Accept: writer_action = 'accepted', modification_delta = '' logged
- Modify: writer_action = 'modified', modification_delta = char diff logged
- Reject: writer_action = 'rejected' logged
- Forbidden word detected: suggestion withheld; warning shown inline
- Ledger entry not deletable: DB trigger raises exception on DELETE attempt
Tags: ai; wga; dialogue; consent; ledger; sprint-6
Parent: AI Service — WGA-Compliant Dialogue Suggestions and Governance

Title: As an org admin AI token quota is enforced per organisation and I can view token consumption analytics
Description: Uncontrolled AI costs are a real risk. Each Professional org has 500K tokens/month enforced via Redis token bucket. Admin dashboard shows real-time consumption, cost estimates, and per-user/per-feature breakdowns. Soft-limit warning at 80% allows proactive management.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Professional quota: 500K tokens/month; 429 returned with Retry-After when exhausted
- Indie tier: all generative features return 403 with upgrade prompt
- Admin dashboard: total tokens consumed, estimated cost (USD), per-user and per-feature tables
- 80% soft limit: warning notification sent to org admin via in-app + email
- Quota resets on the 1st of each month; reset logged in audit trail
- Per-user soft limit (default 50K/month): X-RateLimit-Warning header in response at 90%
Parent: AI Service — WGA-Compliant Dialogue Suggestions and Governance

Title: As a writer I can search all script content and receive relevance-ranked results in under 500ms using hybrid keyword and semantic search
Description: Writers navigate large multi-episode series by intent ('scene where Walter first sees the RV') not just keywords. Hybrid BM25 + semantic search gives far better recall. Cmd+K palette keeps search instantly accessible. Reference: Wiki/10-search.md, ADR-005.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Cmd+K opens search palette from anywhere in the web app
- Results returned in < 500ms (P95) for 100-scene script on staging
- Each result: scene number, heading, matched excerpt with terms highlighted
- Click navigates editor to that scene scrolled into view
- Semantic query ('tense confrontation in industrial setting') returns relevant scene in top 3
- Index updated within 60s of any checkpoint; new/edited scenes immediately searchable
Parent: Search Service — Elasticsearch Hybrid Indexing and Query

Title: Create Atlas DDL schema for breakdown.elements breakdown.categories breakdown.versions and breakdown.element_history tables
Description: In packages/db/schema/breakdown.hcl: (1) breakdown.categories: id UUID PK, script_version_id UUID FK → script.script_versions, scene_id UUID FK → script.ast_nodes, category ENUM(CAST,LOCATION,PROP,WARDROBE,VFX,STUNT,SPECIAL_EQUIPMENT,ANIMAL,VEHICLE,MUSIC,SOUND), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. (2) breakdown.elements: id UUID PK, category_id UUID FK, name TEXT NOT NULL, confidence FLOAT4 DEFAULT 1.0, is_manual BOOL DEFAULT false, is_locked BOOL DEFAULT false, source_text TEXT, notes TEXT, created_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ. (3) breakdown.versions: id UUID PK, script_version_id UUID UNIQUE FK, locked_at TIMESTAMPTZ, locked_by UUID. (4) breakdown.element_history: id UUID PK, element_id UUID FK, action ENUM(CREATED,UPDATED,DELETED), actor UUID, diff JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Indexes: (script_version_id, scene_id, category) on elements; GIN on notes. Run atlas schema apply on staging; verify with psql \\d breakdown.elements.
Parent: As a producer I can see auto-detected breakdown elements for every scene so I can verify and edit them without manual data entry

Title: Implement NLP element detection pipeline for all production element categories using entity recognition and keyword lists
Description: In services/breakdown/src/detection/detector.ts implement BreakdownDetector class. Detection pipeline per scene AST snapshot: (1) Characters: query BibleService.listCharacterNames(projectId) → exact-match against character_name nodes + ALL-CAPS names in action text; create stub for unknowns. (2) Locations: parse scene_heading via regex /^(INT\.|EXT\.|INT\/EXT\.) (.+?) [-—] (.+)$/; extract location name. (3) Props: spaCy en_core_web_sm PRODUCT entities + keyword list (gun, knife, briefcase, phone, bag, car key, etc.). (4) Wardrobe: keyword NER (suit, dress, uniform, badge, tie, coat, mask, etc.). (5) VFX: keyword list (explosion, fire, CGI, digital, miniature, green screen, wire work, etc.). (6) Stunts: keyword list (fight, falls, crash, stunt, dive, chase, physical altercation, etc.). Confidence scoring: Bible exact match = 0.95, keyword match = 0.80, fuzzy match = 0.70. Each detection → { name, category, confidence, source_text }. Unit tests with 5 hand-tagged scenes; assert F1 ≥ 0.80 per category.
Parent: As a producer I can see auto-detected breakdown elements for every scene so I can verify and edit them without manual data entry

Title: Build NATS JetStream consumer that subscribes to script.checkpointed and triggers NLP re-detection for changed scenes
Description: In services/breakdown/src/consumers/checkpointed.consumer.ts: NATS JetStream push consumer on script.checkpointed (durable: breakdown-detector, deliver: new). On message: (1) Parse ScriptCheckpointedPayload { scriptId, versionId, changedSceneIds }. (2) For each changedSceneId: call ScriptService.getSceneSnapshot gRPC. (3) Run BreakdownDetector.detectScene(snapshot). (4) Upsert: skip is_manual = true elements; replace existing auto-detections for this scene. (5) Publish breakdown.updated { scriptId, sceneIds }. (6) Ack message. Error handling: nack with 5s requeue delay on failure; max 3 retries; publish to breakdown.detection.dlq dead-letter queue after exhaustion. SLA: < 30s for a scene with 500 words of action. Integration test: publish mock checkpoint event; assert elements in DB within 35s.
Parent: As a producer I can see auto-detected breakdown elements for every scene so I can verify and edit them without manual data entry

Title: Implement extractBreakdownFromVersion gRPC method callable by Temporal publish-saga activity
Description: In services/breakdown/src/grpc/breakdown.service.ts implement ExtractBreakdownFromVersion RPC. Input: { scriptVersionId: UUID, callerWorkflowId: string }. Output: { breakdownVersionId: UUID, elementCount: number, warnings: string[] }. Logic: (1) Check breakdown.versions for existing record; if exists return it (idempotent). (2) Run full detection across all scenes in the script version. (3) Insert detected elements with is_locked = false. (4) Insert breakdown.versions row with scriptVersionId and callerWorkflowId. (5) Return result. Idempotency: second call with same scriptVersionId returns existing ID without re-running detection. Integration test: call twice with same scriptVersionId; assert second call returns same breakdownVersionId and no duplicate elements.
Parent: As a producer I can see auto-detected breakdown elements for every scene so I can verify and edit them without manual data entry

Title: Build breakdown catalog CRUD gRPC methods with manual override flag and version-lock enforcement
Description: In services/breakdown/src/grpc/breakdown.service.ts implement: (1) CreateBreakdownElement: sets is_manual = true, confidence = 1.0; writes element_history CREATED. (2) UpdateBreakdownElement: returns FAILED_PRECONDITION if is_locked = true; writes element_history UPDATED with JSONB diff. (3) DeleteBreakdownElement: returns FAILED_PRECONDITION if version locked; soft-delete sets deleted_at; writes element_history DELETED. (4) LockBreakdownVersion: sets is_locked = true on all elements in version; sets breakdown.versions.locked_at; called by publish-saga LockBreakdownActivity. All methods: validate PRODUCER or ADMIN role from X-Roles gRPC metadata. Unit tests for each method covering success, not-found, and locked states.
Parent: As a 1st AD I can manually add edit and remove breakdown elements per scene to correct auto-detections

(2) ai.feature_consents: id UUID PK, user_id UUID, feature_type ENUM(dialogue_suggestion,consistency_check,coverage_analysis,structure_analysis), enabled_at TIMESTAMPTZ, disabled_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE(user_id,feature_type). (3) ai.ledger_entries: id UUID PK, org_id UUID, user_id UUID, feature_type TEXT, model_id TEXT, prompt_hash TEXT, context_refs JSONB, ai_output TEXT, writer_action ENUM(accepted,rejected,modified,pending), modification_delta TEXT, cost_usd NUMERIC(10,6), tokens_input INT, tokens_output INT, tokens_cache_read INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(). Title: Build AI context assembler that constructs a cacheable Bible prefix and a fresh per-request suffix for Anthropic prompt caching
Description: In services/ai/src/context/assembler.ts implement assembleContext({ projectId, scriptId, sceneId, characterNames, featureType }): Promise<{ cacheablePrefix, freshSuffix }>. Cacheable prefix (Redis-cached per checkpointHash, TTL 5 min): (1) Project title, genre, logline. (2) Per character: BibleService.getCharacterFacts + getVoiceProfile → format as CHARACTER block with facts, vocabulary level, forbidden words, speech patterns. (3) World rules: BibleService.getWorldRules → bullet list. (4) Reference scenes: getVoiceProfiles → referenceSceneIds → ScriptService.getSceneSnapshot → append up to 3 example dialogue exchanges per character. Fresh suffix (per request): preceding 3 scenes + current scene AST + feature-specific instruction. Redis key: ai:context:prefix:{scriptId}:{checkpointHash}. Unit test: assert cacheablePrefix identical for two calls with same checkpointHash; assert freshSuffix differs for different sceneIds.
Parent: As a writer I can request AI dialogue suggestions with WGA-compliant consent and full ledger tracking

Title: Implement AI model router selecting Claude Haiku Sonnet or Opus by feature tier and org plan

Tier 1 (format_correction, consistency_check) → claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 on all plans. Tier 2 (dialogue_suggestion, structure_analysis) → Professional: claude-sonnet-4-6; Studio: claude-sonnet-4-6. Tier 3 (coverage_analysis) → Studio: claude-opus-4-6; Professional: claude-sonnet-4-6. Indie: Tier 2/3 features return FeatureNotAvailableError with upgrade message. Implement FeatureRegistry map: FeatureType → { tier, requiresConsent, minPlan }. Unit tests: Haiku selected for format_correction on all plans; Sonnet for dialogue_suggestion on Professional; FeatureNotAvailableError for Indie on dialogue_suggestion.

Title: Implement dialogue_suggestion feature end-to-end from consent check through context assembly model call character guard and ledger write
Description: In services/ai/src/features/dialogue-suggestion.ts implement requestDialogueSuggestion({ orgId, userId, projectId, scriptId, sceneId, targetCharacter, promptInstruction? }): Promise<DialogueSuggestionResponse>. Pipeline: (1) checkConsent(orgId, userId, 'dialogue_suggestion') → throw ConsentRequiredError if missing. (2) consumeOrgTokens(orgId, estimatedTokens=1000) → throw QuotaExceededError if not allowed. (3) selectModel + assembleContext. (4) callWithCaching → { content, usage, cost_usd }. (5) Character guard: getVoiceProfile(targetCharacter) → check content against forbidden_words. If match: return { suggestions: [], voiceViolation: { characterName, forbiddenWord } }. (6) writeLedgerEntry with writer_action = 'pending'. (7) consumeOrgTokens with actual token count. (8) Parse 2-3 suggestions from model output. Return { suggestions, ledgerEntryId, voiceViolation? }. Unit tests: happy path, consent missing, quota exceeded, voice violation.
Parent: As a writer I can request AI dialogue suggestions with WGA-compliant consent and full ledger tracking

Title: Implement format_correction feature as an always-available Tier 1 feature using Claude Haiku without consent check
Description: In services/ai/src/features/format-correction.ts implement checkFormatting({ scriptId, sceneId, content }): Promise<FormatCorrectionResponse>. Always Tier 1: no company consent or writer opt-in required; available on all plans including Indie. Model: always claude-haiku-4-5-20251001. Prompt: serialize scene AST to labeled text; instruct model to return JSON array of { elementId: UUID, violationType: string, suggestion: string } for: scene heading format, character name casing, parenthetical overuse, action line length (>4 lines), dialogue line length (>35 chars). writeLedgerEntry with writer_action = 'accepted' (auto-applied). Return { violations }. Test with 3 scenes: one clean, one with heading errors, one with long action.

Title: Implement Redis Lua token bucket rate limiting for org-level monthly quota and per-user soft limits

Title: Create Elasticsearch script_content index mapping with BM25 text fields and dense_vector field for OpenAI embeddings
Description: In services/search/src/indices/script-content.index.ts implement createScriptContentIndex(client): Promise<void>. Settings: shards=2, replicas=1, screenplay_analyzer (standard + english stopwords). Mappings: scene_id (keyword), script_id (keyword), project_id (keyword), episode_id (keyword), scene_number (keyword), heading_text (text, analyzer: screenplay_analyzer, + raw keyword sub-field), action_text (text), dialogue_text (text), character_names (keyword array), scene_type (keyword), updated_at (date), embedding (dense_vector, dims=3072, index=true, similarity=cosine, hnsw: m=16 ef_construction=100). Title: Build NATS consumer that subscribes to script.checkpointed and re-indexes changed scenes with OpenAI embeddings
Description: In services/search/src/consumers/checkpointed.consumer.ts: NATS JetStream push consumer on script.checkpointed (durable: search-indexer). On message: (1) Parse changedSceneIds. (2) ScriptService.getSceneSnapshot gRPC per scene. (3) Build sceneText: heading + action nodes + character names + dialogue nodes. (4) Call openai.embeddings.create({ model: 'text-embedding-3-large', input: sceneText }) → float[3072]. (5) client.update({ index: 'script_content', id: sceneId, doc: { ...sceneFields, embedding }, doc_as_upsert: true }). Rate limit: 10 scenes in parallel; OpenAI rate limit → exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s; max 4 retries). SLA: all changed scenes indexed within 60s. Integration test: publish checkpoint; wait 65s; assert all sceneIds in Elasticsearch.
Parent: As a writer I can search all script content and receive relevance-ranked results in under 500ms

Title: Implement hybrid BM25 and dense vector search using Elasticsearch native RRF combining keyword and semantic scores
Description: In services/search/src/search/hybrid.searcher.ts implement hybridSearch({ projectId, query, limit=20, facets? }): Promise<SearchResult[]>. (1) Embed query via openai (or semantic_cache lookup first). (2) Elasticsearch RRF query: { retriever: { rrf: { retrievers: [ { standard: { query: { multi_match: { query, fields: ['heading_text^3','action_text','dialogue_text','character_names^2'], type: 'best_fields' } } } }, { knn: { field: 'embedding', query_vector: embed, num_candidates: 50, k: limit } } ], rank_constant: 60, window_size: 100 } }, highlight: { fields: { action_text: {}, dialogue_text: {}, heading_text: {} } }, size: limit }. Apply facet bool.filter for project_id + optional scriptIds/episodeIds/sceneTypes/characterNames. Map hits to SearchResult { sceneId, scriptId, sceneNumber, headingText, excerpt, score }. P95 < 500ms assertion in load test.
Parent: As a writer I can search all script content and receive relevance-ranked results in under 500ms

Title: Build Search Service GraphQL subgraph with SearchResult type and searchScripts query with optional facet filters
Description: In services/search/src/graphql/schema.graphql: type SearchResult { sceneId, scriptId, sceneNumber, headingText, excerpt, score }. input SearchFacetInput { scriptIds, episodeIds, sceneTypes, characterNames }. Query: searchScripts(projectId: ID!, query: String!, limit: Int, facets: SearchFacetInput): [SearchResult!]!. Resolver calls hybridSearch. Rate limit: 60 queries/min per user (Redis). Register subgraph in supergraph.yaml. Integration test: index 5 test scenes; query 'junkyard confrontation'; assert correct scene in top 3.
Parent: As a writer I can search all script content and receive relevance-ranked results in under 500ms

Title: Build AI suggestion panel component with request display accept reject modify interactions and ledger update calls
Description: Create apps/web/src/editor/components/AISuggestionPanel.tsx. Right sidebar (360px) toggled by Cmd+Shift+A or toolbar button. State: { suggestions, ledgerEntryId, voiceViolation?, isLoading, error }. Request: call requestAISuggestion mutation with sceneId and targetCharacter (nearest character_name node above cursor). Loading: shimmer skeleton cards. Voice violation: amber banner 'Suggestion withheld — [CHARACTER] voice restriction'. Per suggestion: card with text; Accept (write to editor + acceptSuggestion mutation); Reject (rejectSuggestion mutation); Modify (inline textarea pre-filled; on save call modifySuggestion with final text + client-side char diff). ESC closes panel. Vitest test: render with 3 mocked suggestions; click Accept; assert editor write called + acceptSuggestion mutation fired with correct ledgerEntryId.
Parent: As a writer I can request AI dialogue suggestions with WGA-compliant consent and full ledger tracking

Title: Build AI consent settings UI with per-feature toggle list company consent status and consent history
Description: Create apps/web/src/settings/AIConsentSettings.tsx. Section within UserSettings page. Company consent status row: green tick 'AI features enabled by [orgName]' or amber warning 'Not yet enabled — contact your administrator'. Feature toggle list: Radix UI Switch per feature (dialogue_suggestion, consistency_check, coverage_analysis) with label, description, and last-enabled date. Toggle enable: enableAIFeature mutation. Toggle disable: disableAIFeature mutation. If company consent missing: all toggles disabled with tooltip. Vitest test: render with company consent present; toggle dialogue_suggestion; assert mutation called; assert switch shows enabled.
Parent: As an org admin AI token quota is enforced per organisation and I can view token consumption analytics

Title: Build breakdown panel with scene accordion grouped by category confidence badges manual add form and lock indicator
Description: Create apps/web/src/editor/components/BreakdownPanel.tsx. Right sidebar (320px) for currently selected scene. Apollo useGetBreakdownElementsQuery(sceneId). If locked: 'Locked — version published' banner; all controls hidden. Element list: Radix Accordion one section per populated category. Section header: icon + category name + count badge. Each element row: name, confidence badge (GREEN ≥0.9 / AMBER 0.7-0.9 / RED <0.7), pen icon if is_manual, trash icon (hidden when locked). Click-to-edit inline for name. 'Add Element' button per category: inline form with name input + category select + notes textarea + Save/Cancel. Save calls createBreakdownElement. Delete shows confirmation popover. Vitest test: render with 5 elements across 3 categories; assert locked panel hides edit controls.
Parent: As a producer I can see auto-detected breakdown elements for every scene so I can verify and edit them without manual data entry

Title: Build search command palette with Cmd+K shortcut instant debounced results scene navigation and highlighted excerpts
Description: Create apps/web/src/components/SearchCommandPalette.tsx using cmdk or Radix Dialog. Global Cmd+K (Mac) / Ctrl+K (Win/Linux) via useEffect keydown listener in EditorPage. On open: auto-focus input. Debounced search: 300ms after keystroke; call searchScripts(projectId, query) GraphQL query. Results list: up to 10; each row shows scene number badge + heading text + excerpt with <mark> tags on matched terms (ES highlight field). Arrow keys + Enter to navigate; Enter: scrollToScene(sceneId) dispatch + close palette. Empty state: 'No results for [query]'. Loading: spinner in input right. Min query length: 2 chars. Vitest test: render; type 'junkyard'; assert searchScripts called after 300ms debounce; mock 3 results render; press Enter on first; assert scrollToScene called.
Parent: As a writer I can search all script content and receive relevance-ranked results in under 500ms
